verdict.note: Real-browser session menu: live row shows five icon-led actions; an intercepted offline fixture shows four actions with no attach item or dangling separator. Computed chrome is 11px type, 28px rows, 14px registry icons, with destructive close isolated and right-bottom placement clamped inside the viewport.

Real-browser node menus: an overlay node shows the fixed icon-led commands plus its status-glyph session group and two separators; a plain node shows exactly four fixed commands and one separator. Labels ellipsize cleanly, focus is visible, danger is isolated, and no emoji appear.
5 icon-led rows in 2 groups, separator before the danger group, no emoji, clamped on-screen; menu presses are now pointer-inert (focus stays with the input surface)
4 icon-led rows in 2 groups, separator before danger, no emoji, on-screen; same shared shell, same inert-chrome guard
